- this view is `price_view = raw_usd_view` from the input contract (spec §2)
- One parquet file serves both training and the OOS test — that is why it has no cuts and no features (register C-71).
- We split each ticker's series into three windows (dates = parameters, spec §5a).
- The windows are indices on one continuous series, not separate parquet files (register C-71).
- The detector returns setup objects (contract spec §3).
- Every row gets a `label_uniqueness_weight` weight (formula: spec §6).

## 13. What is canonical vs. reference design (one valid realization)
[quality_gate_spec_eng.md](quality_gate_spec_eng.md); parameters are in [`../config/params.json`](../config/params.json).
The upstream `docs/SPEC.md`, `FLOW/`, and `docs/PIPELINE_REVIEW_CONFIGURABLES.md` are provenance only.
- **Asset** — any instrument with OHLCV data; the pipeline is independent of price scale and timeframe (spec §1).
- **TF (timeframe)** — candle resolution; default `1h` (parameter spec §7).

- **Parameters** — the only place for configuration = `config/params.json` (contract spec §7); zero thresholds hardcoded in the code. The most important: `TF`, `H=24`, `MIN_TOUCHES=2`, `W_ATR=14`, `W_VOL=20`, `ATR_VARIANT=wilder`, `PRICE_VIEW=raw_usd_view`, `THRESHOLD_ENTRY=0.60`, `PURGE_CANDLES=H`,
- **volume required** — for this universe, missing volume = hard fail QC (spec §2).
- **ET-naive → UTC tz-aware conversion** — done only by the F1 reader at the read boundary (spec §2); in the archive the time stays naive.
- **`price_view = raw_usd_view`** — the semantic name of the input price view from the contract (spec §2/§7); one value per dataset (manifest), guarantees a repeatable result.
- **`TOUCH_TOL`** — line-touch tolerance (a detector-contract parameter, defined in F2; dedup: one swing-touch).
- **NaN/Inf** — 0 in Output B apart from documented cases (spec §8).

> **What is certified.** **PART 1 (L0–L3)** is the **CERTIFIED v1** scope — it documents the **real `qc-transforms`** code (`structure/src/.../transform/{l1,l2,l3}/_010_features.py`, driven by `structure/config/materialization_plan_1h_day.json` and `structure/config/project.toml`). **PART 2 (L4 + L5
# PART 2 — reference design — v2 extension, NOT part of the v1 certified implementation

> The whole of PART 2 is a **reference design (one valid realization)** for the v2 extension. It is **NOT** built in v1; the `qc-transforms` materialization plan lists every `l4_*` id under `extensions_not_in_v1_dag_skipped`. The formulas, parameters and APIs below are concrete and build-ready so v2
## L4 — Classical indicators (reference design — v2 extension)
## L5 — Research representations (reference design — v2 extension)

> **Threshold provenance.** The master contract (`docs/SPEC.md`, v1.2) defers the *numeric thresholds* of L8 to roadmap item **F4b**. Every threshold and every invented structural choice in this document is therefore labelled **reference design (one valid realization)**. The *set* of counters/pariti

> **Two pipelines, never conflated.** **Pipeline A** = the L1–L10 strategy pipeline this gate guards. **Pipeline B** = the OHLCV → L5 feature DAG (its own layer scheme L0–L5). L8 is a Pipeline A artifact. Pipeline B appears here only as an **optional upstream-parity extension** (§7), clearly fenced

> **Certified v1 boundary.** Certified v1 = Pipeline A end-to-end (the detector is a *reference implementation* of the `docs/SPEC.md` §3 output contract) + Pipeline B L0–L3 (the real `qc-transforms`). Pipeline B L4/L5 are SPECIFIED but labelled **v2 extension (reference design)** and are **not** par

> P1/P2 numbers (8 841 820 / 503 / 503 files) are **source facts** from `ENG/glossary_eng.md` (L3, L4), **not** reference design. P3 has no fixed numeric target — it is a structural equality (counts derived at build time), so its target is "exact match per partition", not a literal.

All thresholds below are **reference design (one valid realization)** — `docs/SPEC.md` defers the numbers to F4b. They are fixed here so the gate is buildable today; F4b may re-pin them in `config/params.json` (the only configuration site), but the *comparison operators and the structure are frozen*

## 7. v2 extension (reference design) — upstream Pipeline B parity

**Not part of the certified v1 L8 gate.** Certified v1 measures only the Pipeline A chain (§1). When the strategy later consumes engineered features from **Pipeline B** (the OHLCV → L5 feature DAG, `qc_raw_ohlcv_data_sp500_alpaca_transforms`), L8 can be extended with **upstream-parity** checks again

These v2 checks would each get their own `checks[]` entry with the same `{id, level, value, threshold, desc}` shape and feed the same §4 aggregation — but only **after** Pipeline B L4/L5 graduate from "v2 extension (reference design)" to certified.

- All thresholds live in `config/params.json` (single configuration site); the operators and schema in this document are frozen. The *numbers* are **reference design (one valid realization)** pending F4b.
